8pc ASR Outdoor Complete Gold Rush Sifting Classifier Sieve Set for Prospecting
The ASR Outdoor 8pc Complete Gold Classifier Set is a must-have for efficient gold prospecting and material separation. Featuring 8 stainless steel mesh sizes from 1/2" to 1/100", these durable, stackable sieves fit standard 5-gallon buckets and help you quickly sort material for faster, more effective gold and material recovery. Mesh sizes include 1/2", 1/4", 1/8", 1/12", 1/20", 1/30", 1/50", and 1/100".

These sand and gravel screens are made of tough, high-impact plastic and 304 stainless steel wire mesh. Each screen will fit on a standard 5 gallon bucket, and they are stackable!

Although gold is heavy, small flakes will not be able to displace larger rocks from your initial sample. Screening or "classifying" material into groups based on size will ensure that any gold, whether nuggets or flakes, will not get lost.
Key Features
  • MULTIPLE MESH SIZES: 1/2", 1/4", 1/8", 1/12", 1/20", 1/30", 1/50", 1/100"
  • STACKABLE DESIGN: Fits on any standard 3 or 5 gallon bucket, stack multiple classifiers for efficient recovery
  • RUGGED WEATHERPROOF DESIGN: Made from high-impact durable ABS and 304 stainless steel wire mesh, will not rust or corrode under normal wear
  • GOLD PROSPECTING EQUIPMENT: Sift out rocks and soil from your high-yielding pay dirt before working it through a gold pan or sluice box
  • MULTIPURPOSE SIFTING SCREENS: Ideal for many different purposes and a wide range of materials, such as gardening, archaeology, construction, metal detecting, arts and crafts, and more!
Specifications
MPN: ASRO8GCS
UPC: 874211253964
Mesh Sizes: 1/2", 1/4", 1/8", 1/12", 1/20", 1/30", 1/50", 1/100"
Wire Thickness (mm): 1.40, 0.95, 0.60, 0.45, 0.35, 0.30, 0.17, 0.14, 0.10
Material: High Impact ABS and 304 Stainless Steel
Number of Pieces: 8
Type: Classifier Screen Set
In short, gold is heavy, meaning it will sink to the bottom of a gold pan; but, if a gold pan is full of oversized rocks, it messes with that natural settling process. Classifying via sifting screens lets you focus on materials where gold nuggets, flakes or flour will actually show up, improving your chances of catching it. When you classify by size, you can run each size group through your gold panning or sluicing system separately. That gives you better control and increases the odds of recovering fine gold that might get lost in a rough mix.

And, if you're using a sluice, high banker, or other complex gold prospecting equipment, feeding oversized material into it can cause clogs, reduce efficiency, or even damage it. Here, classifiers act like a first line of defense.
